metadata.purpose: derive circuit duties and screen candidate rating classes without hiding missing engineering inputs

continuous.basis: Course-design closure: before a final power-flow study, the 220kV bus-section bay is assigned the N-1 outgoing-line duty.
Fixed course-design feeder current used only for rating-class screening; the exact grounding-transformer steady-state magnetizing current and vendor protection coordination remain pending.
Fixed course-design feeder current used only for rating-class screening; the exact grounding-transformer steady-state magnetizing current and vendor protection coordination remain pending.

availability_evidence.scope: official 252kV outdoor SF6 live-tank family and drawing only; current and breaking ratings not public on the cited page
official family-level availability up to 4000A standard-table current and 63kA; not an exact project configuration

numeric_checks.note: Nominal-voltage class precheck.
Candidate current after one configured correction factor; missing verified correction keeps the final check pending.
Initial symmetrical current is retained as a conservative course precheck at the 0.09s contact-separation assumption.
Course-only peak precheck using the fixed-k converter-inclusive RMS upper bound.
Dynamic course precheck; exact R/X and converter peak treatment remain for final engineering review.
Short-time current-level screen before the explicit I²t comparison.
Course I²t screen uses 1.10s, based on backup protection plus total breaker clearing time rounded upward.

final_checks.note: Nominal-voltage class precheck.
Final voltage-class check.
Candidate current after one configured correction factor; missing verified correction keeps the final check pending.
Verified 41C and installation current correction is not available.

Course-only peak precheck using the fixed-k converter-inclusive RMS upper bound.
Dynamic course precheck; exact R/X and converter peak treatment remain for final engineering review.
Short-time current-level screen before the explicit I²t comparison.
Course I²t screen uses 1.10s, based on backup protection plus total breaker clearing time rounded upward.
Candidate ambient limit and 41C current correction are not verified.
The course-design altitude is 1000m, but the exact candidate service limit is not verified.
Project course assumption is pollution class d; exact candidate external-insulation data are not verified.
Renewable contribution or contact-separation current scope is incomplete.
Renewable dynamic peak and standard peak-factor treatment are incomplete.
Candidate evidence status is target_only; exact model evidence is required.

scope.purpose: close the course-design checks for conductors, surge arresters, CT/PT, insulators, bushings and earthing switches
scope.ordering_boundary: all supplementary rows are course target classes, not purchase specifications or construction release data

check.note: A limiting reactor is not required on the course fault level when the selected switchgear breaking class exceeds the conditional duty.
A limiting reactor is not required on the course fault level when the selected switchgear breaking class exceeds the conditional duty.

current_limiting_reactor.boundary: reassess if the final grid, converter or parallel-operation study exceeds the selected 31.5kA switchgear rating

high_voltage_fuse.scope: only a manufacturer-coordinated primary protection component inside 35kV/10kV VT panels where required
high_voltage_fuse.boundary: fuse type, rating and selectivity belong to the exact VT-panel manufacturer design

checks.note: Normal sectionalized operation keeps one grounding source on each energized section.
Each bus section has one dedicated grounding-transformer and resistor set.
Before bus-tie closure, the receiving or faulted section source is opened and only the healthy-side source remains.
After sectionalized operation is restored, each energized section regains one grounding source.
The bus tie cannot close with both section grounding sources connected in parallel.
Normal sectionalized operation keeps one grounding source on each energized section.

The bus tie cannot close with both section grounding sources connected in parallel.

candidate.final_vendor_boundary: exact zero-sequence impedance, 10s thermal duty, insulation level, losses, temperature rise, CTs, NGR enclosure and protection interfaces require one coordinated manufacturer package

checks.note: Grounding-resistor arithmetic uses nominal line voltage and phase-to-earth voltage U/sqrt(3).
Configured course resistance is checked against Uphase/Ig.
Minimum transformer capacity equals the 10s equivalent power divided by the configured short-time overload factor.
Grounding-transformer feeder switchgear uses the frozen course current target.
One package is provided for each bus section.
Phase CT course target covers the feeder current target; exact burden and saturation remain pending.
Neutral CT course target covers the limited ground-fault current; exact burden and saturation remain pending.
Catalog row is a course target package, not an exact manufacturer model.
Catalog course target uses the same ZN neutral-forming connection.
Course target package retains the required resistor duration.
Catalog target and selected course transformer capacity are aligned.
Catalog target resistance is consistent with Uphase/Ig within the course tolerance.
